steps.title: Identify what would most directly prove the mechanism
steps.content: The hypothesis claims the plants dissolve rock with acids to create growth channels. The most direct evidence would show the plants actively carving into rock.

steps.title: Match evidence to the mechanism
steps.content: C shows the roots carve new channels even when cracks are readily available—proving the plants deliberately bore through rock rather than taking the easier route. This directly demonstrates the acid-dissolving mechanism is active and necessary. A and B don't show the mechanism works; D contradicts the hypothesis by showing the plants thrive without phosphates. That matches choice C — C is correct.
